title: Shoals (SHLS) Q1 Earnings Preview
text: Analysts anticipate challenging results for the solar energy company. They expect revenue decline of 18.3% year-over-year to $74.23 million, representing acceleration of decline from the prior year's 13.6% decrease, and adjusted earnings of $0.04 per share. Shoals beat revenue expectations by 4.9% in the previous quarter but delivered guidance that missed analysts' expectations on full-year EBITDA projections. The company has missed Wall Street's revenue estimates four times over the last two years, suggesting a pattern of underperformance. Renewable energy competitors show mixed results—Generac posted 5.9% revenue growth while Bloom Energy grew 38.6%, though both saw modest stock reactions post-earnings. Covering analysts have generally reconfirmed their estimates over the last 30 days, indicating no major expectation shifts. The average price target stands at $6.06 versus the current $3.75 share price, suggesting potential upside if results stabilize the narrative around the solar sector slowdown.
